title: Conan O’Brien inspires grads, rips Trump administration at 2026 Harvard commencement
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Conan O'Brien delivered a humorous and inspirational speech at the 2026 Harvard commencement, addressing graduates with humility and wit.
O'Brien critiqued the Trump administration's policies on foreign students and joked about suing Harvard over personal grievances.
Sharing personal experiences, O'Brien emphasized adaptability and connection, encouraging graduates to prioritize community over individual achievement.

title: Healey issues new ICE guidance for schools, hospitals, and places of worship
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Governor Healey issues new guidance for schools, hospitals, and worship places on interacting with ICE agents.
Healey's executive order bans ICE civil arrests in state buildings and staging immigration operations on state property.
Massachusetts Legislature is working to finalize a bill preventing ICE in sensitive areas, following Healey's legislation.

title: 3 flee after allegedly trying to climb iconic Boston weather beacon
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Three teens allegedly attempted to climb the Old John Hancock Building's weather beacon Wednesday night, Boston police report.
The teens pushed past a security guard to exit, knocking him down, but he declined medical attention.
Police found no suspects or signs of forced entry; investigation into the incident is ongoing.

title: 21-year-old motorcyclist allegedly went 139 mph in N.H.
author: John Waller
bullets: Johnathin Hutchins, 21, was clocked at 139 mph on a 2006 Suzuki GSX1300R in Swanzey, N.H.
Hutchins was arrested for reckless driving in a 50 mph zone on West Swanzey Road.
He was released on personal recognizance and is scheduled to appear in Keene District Court on July 1.

title: Medford’s Julianne Nicholson, fresh off ‘Paradise,’ is hitting the big screen with Brad Pitt
author: Lauren Daley
bullets: Julianne Nicholson stars as 'Sinatra' in Hulu's 'Paradise', renewed for a third and final season.
Nicholson joins Brad Pitt in A24's 'The Riders', filmed in Greece, Brussels, and Ireland.
Nicholson, an Emmy winner, is predicted for another Emmy nomination for 'Paradise' in 2026.

title: Man arrested in Harvard, MIT dorm break-ins
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: A 31-year-old man was arrested for breaking into dorms at Harvard and MIT.
Authorities charged him with assault, burglary, kidnapping, and unlawful entry with intent to commit a felony.
He is set to be arraigned in Cambridge District Court on Thursday following the incidents in May.

title: Chris Sale admits he was ‘shocked’ by Red Sox firing Alex Cora
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Chris Sale was surprised by the Red Sox's decision to fire manager Alex Cora and his coaching staff.
Sale praised Cora's ability to connect with players, highlighting his effective communication across diverse backgrounds.
Since being traded to the Braves, Sale has excelled with a 2.36 ERA and a Cy Young Award win in 2024.

title: A Mass. Hooters is closing its doors
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: The Hooters in Shrewsbury, Massachusetts, located at 291 Boston Turnpike, announced its closure on Facebook.
Shrewsbury Hooters thanked the community for support and memories, marking the end of its operations.
Hooters is rebranding, with original owners acquiring 140 locations to create a more family-friendly atmosphere.

title: Local obituary: Madison Tesniere, 20-year-old from Raymond, N.H.
author: Boston.com Staff
bullets: Madison Tesniere, 20, passed away unexpectedly on May 23, 2026, in Lawrence, Massachusetts.
Maddi, known for her adventurous spirit, worked as a Med Tech in various New Hampshire health care facilities.
She is survived by her parents, siblings, and extended family; calling hours are on May 29, 2026.

title: Registration to open for World Cup Fan Fest after city unveils 6 watch parties
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Registration for the World Cup Fan Festival at Boston's City Hall Plaza opens Thursday at 2 p.m. through the Boston 26 website.
The free Fan Festival will run from June 12 through June 27, accommodating about 5,000 fans per day, with digital passes required.
Boston will host six World Cup watch parties; locations for some have been announced, additional sites to be revealed later.
